name: Tito's Handmade Vodka
notes: clean, black pepper, sweet corn, citrus peel, mineral, smooth
research: 2025 #1 spirit by volume in multiple US control-state markets (Royal Batch / retail data).
best: Vodka soda, martini, member wants America's top-selling spirit name.
avoid: User wants brown spirits or heavy flavor only.
why: Recommend when the member names Tito's or wants the best-selling US vodka — clean, neutral, cocktail-ready.
catalogLine: Tito's Handmade Vodka · Texas · $28 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: clean, black pepper
blurb: Tito's Handmade — America's best-selling vodka by volume. Clean corn spirit built for soda, tonic, and letting a mild cigar speak.

name: Weller Special Reserve
pairing: Medium cigar, nutty, creamy, subtle ark chocolate
notes: smooth, slightly sweet, caramel, honey, butterscotch, honeysuckle, woodiness, wheated, soft oak
best: Pappy-adjacent request, wheated bourbon curious, approachable upgrade from BT.
avoid: User needs lowest shelf or cask-strength only.
why: Recommend when the member wants wheated bourbon without Pappy pricing — honey, vanilla, soft oak.
catalogLine: Weller Special Reserve · Kentucky · $28 · 90 proof (45.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: vanilla, honey
blurb: Weller Special Reserve — honeyed wheated bourbon on the Buffalo Trace mash bill. The pour people mean when they cannot get Pappy.
awards: Avoid very peppery or intensely full-bodied
sterlon: Cohiba Royale, Padrón 1964 Anniversary Series Maduro, La Gran Llave Maduro Robusto, Mild Connecticut Wrappers

name: Woodford Reserve Kentucky Straight Bourbon
mash: Corn, rye, malted barley (high rye accent)
notes: dried fruit, mint, orange, cocoa, vanilla, tobacco spice, citrus, cinnamon, toffee, caramel, chocolate
best: Member wants polished bourbon between entry and allocated, medium cigar night.
avoid: User needs lowest price point or maximum peat/smoke.
why: Recommend for cocoa-oak balance beside a medium-bodied cigar — step up from Buffalo Trace without collector pricing.
catalogLine: Woodford Reserve Kentucky Straight Bourbon · Kentucky · $38 · 90.4 proof (45.2% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: cocoa, oak
blurb: Woodford Reserve — dried fruit, orange, and cocoa over cinnamon, toffee, and chocolate. Official pot-still profile built for a medium cigar.

name: Woodford Reserve Double Oaked
mash: Corn, rye, malt; second toasted barrel
notes: dark fruit, caramel, honey, chocolate, marzipan, toasted oak, vanilla, hazelnut, apple, spice
best: Member already likes Woodford standard, wants dessert bourbon.
avoid: User wants dry or smoky only.
why: Recommend when toffee and dark chocolate in the glass should match a maduro — double-oak depth.
catalogLine: Woodford Reserve Double Oaked · Kentucky · $65 · 90.4 proof (45.2% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: toffee, dark chocolate
blurb: Woodford Double Oaked — toffee and dark chocolate from a second barrel pass. Dessert bourbon that still belongs beside a serious cigar.

name: WhistlePig 10 Year
mash: High-rye whiskey (Canadian distillate; bottled in Vermont)
notes: allspice, orange peel, anise, oak, char, caramel, vanilla, rye spice, mint, butterscotch
best: User asks for rye, spice-forward cigar, or bourbon drinker ready for rye.
avoid: User dislikes spice heat or wants unpeated Scotch only.
why: Recommend when the member wants to learn rye — pepper and mint over oak without losing body.
catalogLine: WhistlePig 10 Year · Vermont (sourced) · $85 · 100 proof (50% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: pepper, mint
blurb: WhistlePig 10 — allspice and orange peel over caramel, rye spice, and mint with a butterscotch finish. Brand-official 100-proof rye lesson.

name: Wild Turkey 101
mash: Corn, rye, malted barley (Wild Turkey mash)
notes: vanilla, cinnamon, oak, caramel, baking spices, orange peel, rye spice
best: Value bourbon, Old Fashioned base, rye-curious step up from BT.
avoid: User wants wheated or ultra-smooth only.
why: Recommend as the benchmark 101-proof bourbon — spice, caramel, and oak without luxury pricing.
catalogLine: Wild Turkey 101 · Kentucky · $28 · 101 proof (50.5% ABV) · Journey: Novice · Notes: caramel, vanilla
blurb: Wild Turkey 101 — the benchmark Kentucky bourbon at 101 proof. Rye spice and caramel that teach structure without allocated nonsense.

name: The Yamazaki 12 Year
notes: peach, pineapple, grapefruit, clove, candied orange, vanilla, mizunara, coconut, cinnamon
best: Japanese whisky prestige, collector conversation, refined fruit-oak cigar pairing.
avoid: Budget-only or user needs everyday $40 pour.
why: Recommend when Yamazaki name recognition and refined fruit-mizunara pairing are the point.
catalogLine: The Yamazaki 12 Year · Japan · $150 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: peach, pineapple
blurb: Yamazaki 12 — peach, mizunara, and quiet spice. The Japanese single malt everyone asks for by name.

name: Ron Zacapa 23
notes: caramel, vanilla, cacao, butterscotch, toasted hazelnut, toffee, dried pineapple, apricot, nutmeg, leather, tobacco, coffee, cinnamon, ginger
best: Sweet rum luxury, special occasion cigar, member wants rum without Diplomatico repeat.
avoid: User wants white rum or dry spirits only.
why: Recommend when the member wants luxury sweet rum — honey, raisin, and butterscotch beside a premium cigar.
catalogLine: Ron Zacapa 23 · Guatemala · $55 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: honey, raisin
blurb: Ron Zacapa 23 — caramel and butterscotch over apricot, nutmeg, leather, and coffee. Solera luxury rum per distillery tasting profile.
